title: Conjecture 1.5
statement_text: For $n$ even any $2n$ matchings of size $n$ in any graph have a rainbow matching of size $n$, and for $n$ odd any $2n-1$ matchings of size $n$ have a rainbow matching of size $n$.
context_text: Barát, Gyárfás and Sárkőzy considered the rainbow matching problem in general graphs. The conjecture extends Drisko's bipartite theorem to general graphs, distinguishing even and odd $n$. Example 1.8 shows that for $n$ even, $2n-1$ matchings of size $n$ do not always suffice, confirming the case split is necessary.
paper_contribution: Proves the weaker bound that any $3n-2$ matchings of size $n$ in any graph have a rainbow matching of size $n$ (Theorem 1.9).
notes: PDF source — statement appears clean. The abstract uses 'we conjecture' but the body of the paper attributes the conjecture to Barát, Gyárfás and Sárkőzy via citation [7].
paper_title: Large rainbow matchings in general graphs
